Class EventSchema

java.lang.Object
com.example.codebase.EventSchema
public final class EventSchema extends Object
Utility class providing Firestore schema normalization helpers for Event documents.

This class centralizes all logic for safely deserializing and normalizing Event objects loaded from Firestore, including:

  • Resolving missing or mismatched document IDs
  • Ensuring all entrant lists are non-null
  • Deriving a draw date from the registration deadline when absent
  • Defaulting winnersCount to maxCapacity when not explicitly set
  • Converting raw Firestore list values to typed List<String> device ID lists

This is a non-instantiable utility class. All methods are static.

  • Method Details

    • normalizeLoadedEvent

      public static Event normalizeLoadedEvent(com.google.firebase.firestore.DocumentSnapshot documentSnapshot)
      Deserializes and fully normalizes an Event from a Firestore DocumentSnapshot.

      This overload handles the complete deserialization pipeline:

      1. Converts the snapshot to an Event via DocumentSnapshot.toObject(java.lang.Class<T>).
      2. Delegates shared normalization logic to normalizeLoadedEvent(Event, String).
      3. Explicitly re-reads the four entrant lists (waitingList, selectedEntrants, enrolledEntrants, cancelledEntrants) directly from the raw snapshot to handle both legacy Map-based and current String-based Firestore formats via toDeviceIdList(Object).
      Parameters:
      documentSnapshot - the Firestore DocumentSnapshot to deserialize; may be null
      Returns:
      a normalized Event, or null if the snapshot is null, does not exist, or cannot be deserialized

    • normalizeLoadedEvent

      public static Event normalizeLoadedEvent(Event event, String documentId)
      Normalizes an Event object that has already been deserialized from Firestore, applying defaults and repairs for fields that may be missing or inconsistent.

      Normalization steps applied in order:

      1. ID resolution — if the event's id field is absent, it is set from documentId; if it is already present, it is also written to eventId for legacy compatibility.
      2. Entrant lists — any null list (waitingList, selectedEntrants, enrolledEntrants, cancelledEntrants) is replaced with an empty ArrayList.
      3. Draw date — if drawDate is absent, it is calculated as 3 days after registrationDeadline via calculateDrawDate(Date).
      4. Winners count — if winnersCount is absent but maxCapacity is set, winnersCount defaults to maxCapacity.
      Parameters:
      event - the partially deserialized Event to normalize; may be null
      documentId - the Firestore document ID to apply if the event has no ID set; may be null
      Returns:
      the normalized Event, or null if event is null

    • calculateDrawDate

      public static Date calculateDrawDate(Date registrationDeadline)
      Calculates the lottery draw date as 3 days after the given registration deadline.

      This method is used as a fallback when no explicit draw date is stored in Firestore for an event.

      Parameters:
      registrationDeadline - the event's registration close date; may be null
      Returns:
      a Date representing the calculated draw date, or null if registrationDeadline is null

    • toDeviceIdList

      public static ArrayList<String> toDeviceIdList(Object rawValue)
      Converts a raw Firestore list value into a typed ArrayList of device ID strings.

      Firestore may return entrant list entries in one of two formats depending on how the data was originally written:

      • String — the entry is a plain device ID string and is added directly.
      • Map — the entry is a legacy object with a "deviceId" key; the value is extracted and added if it is a non-empty string.

      Entries that are neither a String nor a Map, and Map entries whose "deviceId" value is absent, non-string, or empty, are silently skipped.

      Parameters:
      rawValue - the raw value returned by DocumentSnapshot.get(String); expected to be a List, but safely handles any type
      Returns:
      an ArrayList of non-null, non-empty device ID strings; never null, but may be empty if rawValue is not a List or contains no valid entries
